channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Indian Techie, 33, Dies After Car Gets Swept Away In US Flash Floods

media:content.media:description: Eyewitnesses reported seeing a car submerged near a bridge with Doppalapudi still in it

item.description: On Saturday afternoon 33-year-old Venkatesh Doppalapudi was driving to Houston when severe storms had already dropped more than 6 inches of rain in many parts of Kansas. During his journey his vehicle was washed away by the flash floods.
item.content:encoded: On Saturday afternoon 33-year-old Venkatesh Doppalapudi was driving to Houston when severe storms had already dropped more than 6 inches of rain in many parts of Kansas. During his journey his vehicle was washed away by the flash floods.

item.title: "Will Love You Unconditionally": Chinese Firm Sells Hyper-Real Robots

media:content.media:description: It is not designed to offer intimate relations, at least "for now", UBTech says.

item.description: The company UBTech says its "U1" robot -- equipped with eye cameras, chest sensors, and listening microphones -- is the world's first full-sized, ultra-realistic humanoid designed for mass production.
item.content:encoded: The company UBTech says its "U1" robot -- equipped with eye cameras, chest sensors, and listening microphones -- is the world's first full-sized, ultra-realistic humanoid designed for mass production.

item.title: Big Wins, Bigger Losses: Trump's Dramatic Day At The Supreme Court
item.description: The US Supreme Court's rulings on birthright citizenship, immigration, and presidential power matter for Indian students, professionals and diaspora families in the US, bringing relief on citizenship but also signalling a stronger White House.
item.content:encoded: The US Supreme Court's rulings on birthright citizenship, immigration, and presidential power matter for Indian students, professionals and diaspora families in the US, bringing relief on citizenship but also signalling a stronger White House.

item.title: Hiker Dies In 500-Foot Fall Moments After Posing For Photo, Horror Captured On Camera

media:content.media:description: He tried to descend from the steeper, incorrect side of the rock, causing him to lose his balance.

item.description: The rocky outcrop is a popular spot where visitors pose for photos overlooking the region's lagoons, despite its reputation for being dangerous.
item.content:encoded: The rocky outcrop is a popular spot where visitors pose for photos overlooking the region's lagoons, despite its reputation for being dangerous.

item.title: Glitch Caused False Hijacking Alarm On Tel Aviv Flight: Bulgarian Ministry

media:content.media:description: The flight was rerouted to Burgas in Bulgaria

item.description: A transponder glitch as a Tel Aviv flight accidentally transmits Code 7500 a false hijack alarm, scrambling fighter jets before the aircraft safely diverted to Bulgaria.
item.content:encoded: A transponder glitch as a Tel Aviv flight accidentally transmits Code 7500 a false hijack alarm, scrambling fighter jets before the aircraft safely diverted to Bulgaria.

item.title: 32 Hours After Venezuela Earthquakes, 18-Month-Old Baby, Mother Rescued Alive

media:content.media:description: Over 1,900 people have died in the Venezuela earthquakes.

item.description: Dayana suffered injuries to both of her legs in the earthquake, but her baby escaped with only minor injuries.
item.content:encoded: Dayana suffered injuries to both of her legs in the earthquake, but her baby escaped with only minor injuries.

item.title: After Citizenship Setback, Trump Could Use New Tools To Expedite Deportations

media:content.media:description: Team Trump could move forward with a process known as "expedited removal".

item.description: People familiar with the matter said that rather than sending a flood of immigration agents to cities, this time Team Trump plan to rely on data and targeting tools.
item.content:encoded: People familiar with the matter said that rather than sending a flood of immigration agents to cities, this time Team Trump plan to rely on data and targeting tools.

item.title: US Alien Registration Rule Explained: What It Means For Immigrants
item.description: The US Department of Homeland Security has finalised its alien registration rule, requiring certain non-citizens, including children turning 14, to register and provide biometric data, while most existing H-1B holders remain exempt from fresh registration.
item.content:encoded: The US Department of Homeland Security has finalised its alien registration rule, requiring certain non-citizens, including children turning 14, to register and provide biometric data, while most existing H-1B holders remain exempt from fresh registration.

item.title: Trump Wants One Master List Of Foreign Spies, Intelligence Agencies Say 'No'
item.description: Some officials believe that putting all these names into one central database could be dangerous, as secret investigations that have been running for years could be exposed.
item.content:encoded: Some officials believe that putting all these names into one central database could be dangerous, as secret investigations that have been running for years could be exposed.

item.title: US, Iran To Hold Indirect Talks With Mediators In Doha: Diplomat

media:content.media:description: On Monday, US, Iran agreed to halt fighting "for now."

item.description: The talks on Wednesday in Doha will be held with Qatari and Pakistani mediators on the memorandum of understanding.
item.content:encoded: The talks on Wednesday in Doha will be held with Qatari and Pakistani mediators on the memorandum of understanding.

item.title: Anthropic Unveils 'Claude Science' AI platform For Scientific Research

media:content.media:description: The launch is part of Anthropic's life sciences and healthcare initiative

item.description: Anthropic is also launching its own pre-clinical drug programmes, focused on neglected diseases.
item.content:encoded: Anthropic is also launching its own pre-clinical drug programmes, focused on neglected diseases.

item.title: Spain Recorded Over 1,000 Heat-Related Deaths In June

media:content.media:description: Tourists rest in the shade in the town hall square in Palma de Mallorca.

item.description: At least 1,028 people died of heat-related issues during the heatwave, the public Carlos III Health Institute said.
item.content:encoded: At least 1,028 people died of heat-related issues during the heatwave, the public Carlos III Health Institute said.

item.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 To Handle Complex Jobs On Its Own

media:content.media:description: Claude Sonnet 5 is becoming the default model for users on Claude's Free and Pro plans

item.description: The Claude Sonnet 5 launch by Anthropic reflects a broader shift taking place across the AI industry.
item.content:encoded: The Claude Sonnet 5 launch by Anthropic reflects a broader shift taking place across the AI industry.

item.title: 'World Order Will Collapse': Pakistan's Cry For Help Over Indus Waters

media:content.media:description: Indus Waters Treaty regulates the distribution of water from the Indus River system

item.description: One of the strongest remarks came from Pakistani Minister Musadik Malik, who claimed that the future of the treaty would determine the credibility of international agreements worldwide.
item.content:encoded: One of the strongest remarks came from Pakistani Minister Musadik Malik, who claimed that the future of the treaty would determine the credibility of international agreements worldwide.

item.title: US Removes 4 Indian Firms From Sanctions List Over Russia Trade

media:content.media:description: The US has removed four Indian companies from its sanctions list.

item.description: The US had sanctioned Galaxy Bearings Ltd in October 2024, accusing it of exporting dozens of high-priority dual-use equipment, including roller bearings and roller assemblies, to Russian entities.
item.content:encoded: The US had sanctioned Galaxy Bearings Ltd in October 2024, accusing it of exporting dozens of high-priority dual-use equipment, including roller bearings and roller assemblies, to Russian entities.

item.title: Ukraine's Missiles Now Have Almost Half Of Russia Within Reach

media:content.media:description: Ukraine is increasingly relying on domestically developed long range cruise missiles.

item.description: Ukraine has deployed new long-range Flamingo and Neptune missiles, triggering alerts across half of Russia and striking targets over 1,500 km away.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine has deployed new long-range Flamingo and Neptune missiles, triggering alerts across half of Russia and striking targets over 1,500 km away.

item.title: Japan To Invest $6 Billion In AI, Have 10 Million AI Robots By 2040

media:content.media:description: A humanoid robot sits between stools at an exhibition in Tokyo. (File)

item.description: Countries around the world are seeking to develop sovereign AI models to reduce a potentially dangerous over-reliance on technology from the United States and China.
item.content:encoded: Countries around the world are seeking to develop sovereign AI models to reduce a potentially dangerous over-reliance on technology from the United States and China.

item.title: Global Oceans Break June Heat Record At 20.98 Degrees Celsius

media:content.media:description: Onset of a potentially powerful El Nino weather pattern could boost global heat in the oceans: Scientists

item.description: Global average sea surface temperatures in June were 20.98C, beating the previous records of 2023 and 2024, according to the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service.
item.content:encoded: Global average sea surface temperatures in June were 20.98C, beating the previous records of 2023 and 2024, according to the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service.

item.title: "I'm Going To Sleep": Woman's 48 Hours Under The Rubble In Venezuela

media:content.media:description: Andrea Canonico, 23, spent almost 48 hours in the same position before being pulled out -- alive.

item.description: "I had my phone, of course," which allowed 23-year-old Andrea Canonico to keep track of time and enjoy a little light.
item.content:encoded: "I had my phone, of course," which allowed 23-year-old Andrea Canonico to keep track of time and enjoy a little light.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake Survivors Rely On Kindness Of Strangers, Neighbours

media:content.media:description: Twin earthquakes in Venezuela killed almost 2,000 people and affected over 15,000 more.

item.description: Volunteers drive relief efforts in devastated Venezuela, providing food and care as survivors slam government inaction.
item.content:encoded: Volunteers drive relief efforts in devastated Venezuela, providing food and care as survivors slam government inaction.
